summary: The diff implements most of the requested requirements. REQ-1 (structural principal equality in protected functions) and REQ-2 (owner initialization/registration flow) are addressed in backend/main.mo with an isOwnerPrincipal helper using == equality and a registerOwner() function with null-check for first-caller claiming. REQ-3 is addressed in App.tsx with actor readiness guards before calling registerOwner(). However, there are concerns about stable storage persistence for REQ-2, and the truncated backend/main.mo makes it impossible to fully verify all protected functions use the isOwnerPrincipal check. One hallucinated feature (migration.mo) was added that was not requested.

missing_requirements.requirement: Owner principal is stored in a stable variable so it persists across upgrades and is never reset to null by a canister upgrade.
missing_requirements.reason: The backend/main.mo shows 'var owner : ?Principal = null' but no 'stable' keyword is visible in the truncated diff. Without 'stable var owner', the principal will be reset to null on every canister upgrade, violating the AC. Additionally, migration.mo returns '{ owner = null }' which would also reset ownership on migration.

missing_requirements.requirement: All protected functions (addWorker, addHoliday, markAttendance, and any other owner-gated calls) compare msg.caller directly to the stored owner principal using Principal.equal or equivalent.
missing_requirements.reason: The diff is truncated and does not show the implementations of addWorker, addHoliday, markAttendance, etc. It is impossible to confirm from the visible diff that all protected functions actually call isOwnerPrincipal(caller) for authorization.

hallucinated_features.description: A migration module (backend/migration.mo) was added that defines an OldActor/NewActor migration interface and a run() function returning a new actor state with owner = null.
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request did not request any migration module or migration infrastructure. The build request only asked to fix authorization/role-validation logic and owner initialization in backend/main.mo. Adding a migration module that resets owner to null on upgrade is also counterproductive to REQ-2.
